text: Each Spectra object contains an implementation of a MsBackend within its @backend slot which provides the MS data to the Spectra object.
source_excerpt:
Each `Spectra`
object contains an implementation of such a `MsBackend` within its `@backend`
slot which provides the MS data to the `Spectra` object.

text: The MsBackend virtual class defines the API that new backend classes need to implement in order to be used with the Spectra object.
source_excerpt:
The `MsBackend` virtual class defines the API that new *backend* classes
need to implement in order to be used with the `Spectra` object.

text: Each backend must provide a minimum required set of core spectra variables listed by the coreSpectraVariables() function.
source_excerpt:
a minimum required set of spectra variables
  **must** be provided by each backend (even if their values are empty). These
  *core spectra variables* are listed (along with their expected data type) by
  the `coreSpectraVariables()` function.

text: dataStorage and dataOrigin are special spectra variables that define where the data is stored and from where the data derived.
source_excerpt:
`dataStorage` and `dataOrigin` are two special spectra variables that define
  for each spectrum where the data is stored and from where the data derived (or
  was loaded, such as the data origin).

text: Both dataStorage and dataOrigin must be of type character and need to be defined by the backend.
source_excerpt:
Both are expected to be of
  type`character` and need to be defined by the backend (i.e., they can not be
  empty or missing).

text: MsBackend implementations can represent purely read-only data resources where only data accessor methods need to be implemented but not data replacement methods.
source_excerpt:
`MsBackend` implementations can also represent purely *read-only* data
  resources. In this case only data accessor methods need to be implemented but
  not data replacement methods.

text: Whether a backend is read-only can be set with the @readonly slot of the virtual MsBackend class.
source_excerpt:
Whether a backend is read-only can be set with
  the `@readonly` slot of the virtual `MsBackend` class

text: The default value for @readonly is FALSE, requiring all data replacement methods to be implemented.
source_excerpt:
The default is
  `@readonly = FALSE` and thus all data replacement method listed in section
  *Data replacement methods* have to be implemented.

text: For read-only backends (@readonly = TRUE) only the methods in the Required methods section need to be implemented.
source_excerpt:
For read-only backends
  (`@readonly = TRUE`) only the methods in section *Required methods* need to be
  implemented.

text: The spectraData() method should return the full spectra data within a backend as a DataFrame object.
source_excerpt:
The `spectraData()` method should return the **full** spectra data within a
backend as a `DataFrame` object (defined in the `r Biocpkg("S4Vectors")`
package).

text: Each row in the DataFrame returned by spectraData() should represent one spectrum and each column a spectra variable.
source_excerpt:
Each row in this
data frame should represent one spectrum, each column a spectra
variable.

text: Columns mz and intensity (if requested) must contain each a NumericList with the m/z and intensity values of the spectra.
source_excerpt:
Columns `"mz"` and `"intensity"` (if requested) have to contain each a
`NumericList` with the m/z and intensity values of the spectra.

text: The DataFrame returned by spectraData() must provide values for all requested spectra variables of the backend including the core spectra variables.
source_excerpt:
The `DataFrame`
**must** provide values (even if they are `NA`) for **all** requested spectra
variables of the backend (**including** the core spectra variables).

text: The spectraVariables() method should return a character vector with the names of all available spectra variables of the backend.
source_excerpt:
The `spectraVariables()` method should return a `character` vector with the
names of all available spectra variables of the backend.

text: Each MsBackend class must provide the core spectra variables in the correct data type.
source_excerpt:
each
`MsBackend` class **must** provide also the *core spectra variables* (in the
correct data type).

text: Core spectra variables can be NA with the exception of the spectra variable dataStorage.
source_excerpt:
they can however also be `NA` (with the exception of the
spectra variable `"dataStorage"`).

text: The backendInitialize() method is expected to be called after creating an instance of the backend class and should prepare the backend.
source_excerpt:
The `backendInitialize()` method is expected to be called after creating an
instance of the backend class and should prepare (initialize) the backend

text: During backendInitialize() the special spectra variables dataStorage and dataOrigin are usually set.
source_excerpt:
During `backendInitialize()` usually also the special spectra
variables `dataStorage` and `dataOrigin` are set.

text: peaksData() is expected to return a List of numerical matrices with columns being the requested peaks variables of one spectrum.
source_excerpt:
These are
expected to be returned as a `List` of numerical matrices with columns in each
`matrix` being the requested *peaks variables* (with the default being `"mz"`
and `"intensity"`) of one spectrum.

text: Backends must provide at least mz and intensity as peaks variables.
source_excerpt:
Backends must provide at least these two
peaks variables.

text: The extractByIndex() and [ methods allow to subset MsBackend objects.
source_excerpt: The `extractByIndex()` and `[` methods allows to subset `MsBackend` objects.

text: Subsetting with extractByIndex() and [ must support duplication and extraction in any arbitrary order.
source_excerpt:
These methods must also support duplication (e.g. `[c(1, 1, 1)]` and
extraction in any arbitrary order (e.g. `[c(3, 1, 5, 3)]`).

text: extractByIndex() only supports to subset with an integer index while [ should support to subset by indices or logical vectors.
source_excerpt:
While both methods
subset the object, `extractByIndex()` only supports to subset with an `integer`
index, while `[`, to be compliant with the base R implementation, should support
to subset by indices or logical vectors.

text: An error should be thrown if indices are out of bounds but the method should support returning an empty backend with [integer()].
source_excerpt:
An error should be thrown if indices
are out of bounds, but the method should also support returning an empty backend
with `[integer()]`.

text: The backendMerge() method merges MsBackend objects of the same type into a single instance.
source_excerpt:
The `backendMerge()` method merges (combines) `MsBackend` objects (of the same
type!) into a single instance.

text: The spectraNames() method can be used to extract optional names or IDs for individual spectra of a backend, or NULL if not set.
source_excerpt:
The `spectraNames()` can be used to extract (optional) names (or IDs) for
individual spectra of a backend, or `NULL` if not set.

text: With the $<- method, the length() of value must match the number of spectra represented by the backend.
source_excerpt:
As with all replacement
methods, the `length()` of `value` has to match the number of spectra
represented by the backend.

text: The spectraData<- method should allow to replace the data within a backend by taking a DataFrame with the full data as input value.
source_excerpt:
The `spectraData<-` method should allow to replace the data within a
backend. The method should take a `DataFrame` with the full data as input value

text: The spectraData<- method is expected to replace the full data within the backend including all spectra variables and peak data.
source_excerpt:
and is expected to replace the **full** data within the backend, i.e. all
spectra variables as well as peak data.

text: The number of spectra before and after calling spectraData<- method on an object has to be the same.
source_excerpt:
Also, importantly, the number of spectra
before and after calling the `spectraData<-` method on an object has to be the
same.

text: The intensity<- method should allow to replace only the values of the intensities but must not change the number of intensities per spectrum.
source_excerpt:
The `intensity<-` method should allow to replace the intensity values of all
spectra in a backend. This method is expected to only replace the *values* of
the intensities, but must not change the number of intensities (and hence peaks)
of a spectrum

text: The peaksData<- method should support changing the number of peaks per spectrum such as due to filtering.
source_excerpt:
In contrast to the `mz<-` and `intensity<-`
methods this method should also support changing the number of peaks per
spectrum (e.g. due to filtering).

text: The selectSpectraVariables() function should allow to reduce the information within the backend to the selected spectra variables.
source_excerpt:
The `selectSpectraVariables()` function should allow to reduce the information
within the backend (parameter `object`) to the selected spectra variables
(parameter `spectraVariables`).

text: The selectSpectraVariables() is equivalent to a subset by columns or variables.
source_excerpt:
This is equivalent to a subset by
columns/variables.

text: The $ method is expected to extract a single spectra variable from a backend.
source_excerpt:
The `$` method is expected to extract a single spectra variable from a
backend.

text: Each MsBackend must support extracting the core spectra variables with the $ method even if no data might be available for that variable.
source_excerpt:
Each `MsBackend` **must** support extracting the core spectra variables
with this method (even if no data might be available for that variable).

text: The $ method should check if the requested spectra variable is available and should throw an error otherwise.
source_excerpt:
Also, the `$` method should check if the requested spectra variable is
available and should throw an error otherwise.

text: The peaksVariables() is expected to return a character vector with the names of the peaks variables available in the backend.
source_excerpt:
The `peaksVariables()` is expected to return a `character` vector with the names
of the *peaks variables* (i.e. information and properties of individual mass
peaks) available in the backend.

text: The default implementation for peaksVariables() returns c("mz", "intensity") for MsBackend.
source_excerpt:
The default implementation for `MsBackend`
returns by default `c("mz", "intensity")`.
